title: Audio evidence is a non-collapsible claim ladder

observed: The continuity index already separates evidence identity, provenance, trusted-builder acceptance, and underlying claim truth.
Frontier Research item R-027 separately classifies browser-bound output routing, render activity, physical acoustic output, and human audibility.
R-027 reports that a fulfilled setSinkId request with a matching observed sinkId and enumerated audiooutput membership can support only a browser-bound routing claim.

R-027 explicitly states that browser-bound routing does not prove continuing device availability, acoustic output, or human audibility, and that an empty sinkId identifies only the user-agent default route.
inferred: The project's use of the word 'audio works' has become too coarse for durable evidence records.
Future audio reports require an explicit layer label so that success at one layer is not promoted into success at a stronger layer.
proposed: Use the ordered vocabulary activation_observed, context_running, render_advancing, route_bound, physical_output_observed, and human_audibility_reported.
Record each layer independently with its own source, time, environment, and falsification route.
Treat disagreement between layers as preserved contradiction evidence rather than averaging the observations into one status.
superseded: Any earlier unqualified statement that a running AudioContext, advancing render clock, selected sink, or successful route request alone proves that a person heard sound.
unresolved: No immutable production deployment and physical iPhone Safari observation currently joins all audio evidence layers in one retained packet.
Browser-visible route evidence cannot establish the continued availability or acoustic behavior of the selected physical device.

claim_ladder.proves: A qualifying user activation was observed.
The Web Audio context reported a running state.
Browser-visible audio rendering advanced across retained samples.
The browser reported a bound output route under the recorded API conditions.
A device-level or acoustic measurement observed output under recorded conditions.
does_not_prove: human audibility for a specific person

claim_ladder.proves: A human reported hearing or not hearing sound under recorded conditions.
does_not_prove: which internal browser or device layer caused the outcome

falsification_or_review_route: Fetch the indexed continuity blob and the R-027 handoff commit. Reject any observed statement not supported by those exact sources. Revise an individual claim-ladder layer only when a later standards-linked artifact explicitly changes what that layer can establish; do not erase prior historical wording.
